presentationFlow.title: The Defender portal unified SecOps experience
presentationFlow.talkTrack: Microsoft Sentinel is GA in the Defender portal and retires from the Azure portal March 31, 2027. New customers after July 1, 2025 auto-onboard. The workspace, rules, workbooks, and playbooks in this lab work identically across both portals.
showInPortal: Advanced hunting across Sentinel + Defender XDR tables

presentationFlow.title: Three connectors stream Purview into Sentinel
presentationFlow.talkTrack: Microsoft Defender XDR (kind: MicrosoftThreatProtection) brings DLP alerts via the XDR pipeline. Microsoft 365 Insider Risk Management (kind: OfficeIRM) brings IRM high-severity alerts. Office 365 (kind: Office365) brings unified audit activity. Between them, full Purview signal coverage.
showInPortal: Sentinel > Data connectors > Microsoft Defender XDR
Sentinel > Data connectors > Microsoft 365 Insider Risk Management
presentationFlow.expertCallout: The correct IRM connector kind is OfficeIRM — not MicrosoftPurviewInformationProtection. The latter is for the Information Protection connector (different product).

presentationFlow.title: Analytics rules: signal to incident
presentationFlow.talkTrack: Four rules turn raw signal into investigable incidents. PVSentinel-HighSevDLP catches high-severity DLP alerts. PVSentinel-IRMHighSev catches IRM escalations. PVSentinel-LabelDowngrade catches pre-exfiltration label stripping. PVSentinel-MassDownloadAfterDLP correlates across tables.
showInPortal: Sentinel > Analytics > PVSentinel-HighSevDLP (rule logic + entity mappings)

expectedAuditRecordSample.AlertName: Microsoft 365 DLP - SSN detected in email
expectedAuditRecordSample.Description: Sensitive information type 'U.S. Social Security Number (SSN)' detected in email to external recipient.

presentationFlow.title: IRM auto-triage playbook
presentationFlow.talkTrack: A Logic App triggered by Sentinel incidents appends enrichment context automatically. Managed identity (no stored creds), Microsoft Sentinel Responder role on the workspace, Logic App Contributor granted to the Sentinel first-party app. Incident fires, comment appears in under a second.
showInPortal: Sentinel > Automation > automation rule wiring IRMHighSev → playbook

presentationFlow.title: Purview Signals workbook
presentationFlow.talkTrack: DLP alert volume, IRM severity distribution, top label downgrades by user, cross-table users with both DLP and IRM signals. Executive-ready dashboard for when the question is 'how is our data leaving?'

presentationFlow.title: Complementary stories
presentationFlow.talkTrack: Microsoft Purview Content Hub solution adds MS-maintained analytics rules. DSPM for AI, Shadow AI, and Copilot DLP labs all stream into the same workspace. Sentinel data lake tier handles high-volume table cost optimization. Security Copilot for Purview is the AI-on-AI layer.
showInPortal: Sentinel > Content Hub > Microsoft Purview solution
Defender portal > Sentinel > Tables (data lake tier config)

scenarios.name: DLP → Sentinel incident
scenarios.description: Send an email with SSN, watch it flow through DLP → Defender XDR → Sentinel analytics rule → incident
steps: Run Invoke-SmokeTest.ps1 to send seed email containing SSN 123-45-6789
Wait 15-30 min for DLP + Defender XDR pipeline propagation
Open Sentinel > Incidents > filter to PVSentinel-HighSevDLP
Show incident with entity-mapped user account
Pivot to SecurityAlert table, show the raw alert that triggered it
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Audience sees the full signal chain from M365 email to Sentinel incident

scenarios.name: IRM auto-triage
scenarios.description: Trigger an IRM high-severity alert, watch the playbook auto-enrich the Sentinel incident
steps: Generate IRM signal via Invoke-SmokeTest.ps1 or manual risky behavior on test account
IRM scores user to Elevated (or manually escalate)
Alert flows to Sentinel via OfficeIRM connector (requires SIEM export enabled)
PVSentinel-IRM-AutoTriage playbook fires, comment appears on incident
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Audience sees auto-triage — SOC analyst gets triage context before reading the alert
manualPrep: Insider Risk > Settings > Export alerts = On
60-90 min for first IRM alert to flow

scenarios.name: Cross-table correlation
scenarios.description: Demonstrate the MassDownloadAfterDLP rule catching a multi-signal scenario
steps: User A triggers a DLP match (email with SSN) — creates SecurityAlert row
Within 4 hours, User A downloads 50+ files from SharePoint — generates OfficeActivity rows
MassDownloadAfterDLP rule joins the tables, creates high-severity incident
Show the incident with both SecurityAlert and OfficeActivity entities
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Audience sees that signals noise-on-their-own become signal-together
manualPrep: Generate both signals from same user within the 4h window

scenarios.name: Defender portal migration preview
scenarios.description: Show the same workspace in the Defender portal to preview the unified SecOps experience
steps: Show advanced hunting querying both Sentinel and Defender XDR tables
Show blast-radius analysis (preview) for an incident
scenarios.expectedOutcome: Audience sees Microsoft's recommended SIEM experience
manualPrep: Workspace must be onboarded to Defender portal — see RUNBOOK section 4

userExperienceTable.sentinelBehavior: SecurityAlert row appears within 15-30 min, HighSevDLP rule creates incident within the next hour
Alert flows via OfficeIRM within 60 min of SIEM export enabled, IRMHighSev rule creates incident, playbook enriches
OfficeActivity row, LabelDowngrade rule creates medium-severity incident, entity mapped to user

userExperienceTable.scenario: DLP + mass download same user within 4h
userExperienceTable.sentinelBehavior: MassDownloadAfterDLP correlates across tables, creates high-severity incident with both entities
